goal: Calibrate a Franka actuator-net to MEASURED hardware behaviour by fitting a NEW LSTM model to real-robot joint logs and validating the prediction error on a held-out test split. UNLIKE CP-NEW-actuator-net-training (which records a from-scratch excitation directly in sim to demonstrate the training pipeline), this canonical assumes the operator already has REAL hardware logs (a torque-vs-angle CSV exported from a physical Franka teleop session or factory run); the locally-runnable form ships with a mock CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_hardware_log_mock.csv that emulates the real-log schema with a synthetic friction/backlash noise model so the canonical can execute end-to-end without a physical robot. robot_wizard loads the Franka. create_calibration_experiment is used to register the calibration job + split the loaded log into 80/20 train/test by trajectory index (NOT by row — splitting by row leaks within-trajectory dynamics into the test set). train_actuator_net fits an LSTM (hidden_dim=32, num_layers=2, num_epochs=200) on the TRAIN partition, writing the checkpoint to workspace/calibration/franka_actuator_net_calibrated. validate_calibration computes per-joint RMSE on the held-out TEST partition. calibrate_physics applies the learned damping/friction deltas to the live articulation as an optional post-fit step. suggest_parameter_adjustment annotates any joint whose validation RMSE remains above the 0.05 rad threshold. evaluate_reward replays a fixed reach-to-pose using the calibrated actuator model to confirm the model installs cleanly into the policy-evaluation harness. SUCCESS: validate_calibration reports per_joint_rmse <= 0.05 rad on all 7 joints AND the trained checkpoint is non-null AND no joint's test-RMSE exceeds 2x its train-RMSE (catches train/test leakage and under-fitting). Exercises the actuator-net CALIBRATION pipeline (load real log, split, fit, validate, deploy) — the complementary workflow to CP-NEW-actuator-net-training (from-scratch sim-data demo) and CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (parametric Bayesian fit, not neural). Realizes sim-to-real engineer / hardware-fidelity engineer persona.
thoughts: Pattern is rl-train / actuator-net CALIBRATION (not from-scratch training). Distinguishing axis from A62 (CP-NEW-actuator-net-training): A62 RECORDS its own excitation trajectory in sim via record_trajectory, then trains an LSTM to demonstrate the training pipeline exists. A110 ASSUMES the operator already has real hardware logs, LOADS them from CSV/HDF5, splits into train/test, and validates that the fitted LSTM generalizes. The training step is identical (both call train_actuator_net) but the input data provenance and the validation discipline differ. simulate_args is null — success is RMSE thresholds, not cube-at-destination. verify_args is null — no scene-build verification stage.

Locally-runnable: the canonical ships with a mock CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_hardware_log_mock.csv. Schema mirrors a real teleop export — columns: timestamp_s, joint_0_pos..joint_6_pos, joint_0_vel..joint_6_vel, joint_0_tau..joint_6_tau, episode_id. Synthesized with a deterministic Coulomb-friction + backlash + measurement-noise model so the canonical exercises the LOAD path end-to-end without a physical Franka in the loop. Real-log users substitute their own CSV path verbatim.

Train/test SPLIT BY EPISODE_ID, not by row: splitting a single trajectory's rows into 80%/20% leaks the within-trajectory dynamics (the LSTM sees position(t-k) in both partitions, trivially predicts position(t)). Splitting by episode_id treats whole trajectories as atomic, so the test set contains motion patterns the model has never seen. create_calibration_experiment is the right tool to register this split because its parameter='train_test_split_strategy', range=[0.0, 1.0] with num_samples=2 emits one train-fraction = 0.8 trial — used here for its split-bookkeeping side effect, not its sweep semantics.

train_actuator_net default hyperparams (hidden_dim=32, num_layers=2, num_epochs=200) are correct for the mock CSV size (~24k samples). On a real-log dataset >100k samples, increase num_epochs to 400; if validation RMSE plateaus above the 0.05 threshold, increase hidden_dim to 64 (catches stiffer nonlinearities).

validate_calibration is repurposed here for actuator-net validation rather than parametric-physics validation. The handler accepts calibrated_params as a dict — for actuator-net, this dict carries {'checkpoint_path': ..., 'model_type': 'actuator_net_lstm'}. test_data_path points at the held-out 20% partition. Output schema: per_joint_rmse (rad), mean_rmse, ee_cartesian_rmse — same as parametric calibration, so downstream tooling (suggest_parameter_adjustment, measure_sim_real_gap) consumes both the same way.

Validation threshold: per_joint_rmse <= 0.05 rad (~2.9°). This is the standard tolerance for Franka joint-tracking sim-to-real gap; tighter than 0.02 rad would require a larger network or longer training; looser than 0.1 rad fails to catch under-fitting.

Train/test leakage detector: a model with very low train RMSE but >2x test RMSE is over-fitting to within-trajectory dynamics. The diagnose_args success_criterion enforces test_rmse <= 2 * train_rmse to catch silent leaks. If this fires, the operator should re-inspect the split strategy.

calibrate_physics is called AFTER actuator-net training for users who want to also fit parametric damping/friction deltas as a cross-check. The two approaches are complementary: actuator-net learns the residual; calibrate_physics fits the dominant parametric structure. If only one is needed, the actuator-net call alone is sufficient — the calibrate_physics step is annotated as optional in the code.

evaluate_reward at the end verifies the trained actuator-net installs cleanly into the IsaacLab Gymnasium env without crashing — a smoke test, not a performance gate. The 200-step rollout is short enough to complete inline; longer rollouts go in a separate downstream canonical.

monitor_joint_effort is included to surface a residual diagnostic: after the calibrated actuator-net is loaded, the per-joint effort signal should match the training distribution. If joint_3 sees 10x the effort the training data shows, the calibration mode is suspect — operator should re-check the CSV column mapping.
motion_controllers.untested contains direct_joint because the calibration workflow uses open-loop joint targets, no closed-loop planner. No cuRobo or RMPflow involvement.

Distinct from CP-NEW-actuator-net-training (from-scratch sim-data demo, no train/test split), CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (parametric Bayesian fit on HDF5), CP-NEW-sim2real-gap (single rosbag replay), and CP-NEW-sim2real-gap-injection (controlled-noise sensitivity sweep). This is the ONLY canonical that loads a REAL-HARDWARE log, fits a NEW actuator-net, AND validates on a held-out test split.
code:
# ── Step 1: Load Franka articulation ─────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.0],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Step 2: Register calibration experiment with 80/20 train/test split ─────
# Real hardware log path (mock CSV ships with the canonical for locally-runnable form).
# In a production run, substitute workspace/calibration/franka_hardware_log_mock.csv
# with the operator's real CSV/HDF5 export from a physical Franka teleop session.
_hardware_log = "workspace/calibration/franka_hardware_log_mock.csv"
_train_split_path = "workspace/calibration/franka_train_split.hdf5"
_test_split_path = "workspace/calibration/franka_test_split.hdf5"

# create_calibration_experiment is used here for its split-bookkeeping side effect.
# The parameter axis (train_fraction) records the 0.8/0.2 split as a calibration run.
create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="train_test_split_strategy",
    range=[0.0, 1.0],
    real_data_path=_hardware_log,
    num_samples=2,
)
# Returns: {best_value: 0.8, sweep_results: [{param_value: 0.8, ...}, {param_value: 0.2, ...}]}
# Side effect: writes _train_split_path (80%, by episode_id) and _test_split_path (20%, held out).

# ── Step 3: Verify the loaded log has non-trivial effort signal ─────────────
# If all torque columns are zero, the CSV is malformed (or the source robot's
# drives were off during logging). Catch this BEFORE spending 8 min on training.
_effort_report = monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    duration_seconds=2.0,
)
# Returns: {per_joint_effort_stats: {joint_name: {mean, max, min}}, sample_count: int}
# GATE: at least one |mean_effort| > 0.01 N·m; all zeros => drives disabled or
# CSV column mapping wrong (e.g. joint_0_tau column was loaded as joint_0_pos).

# ── Step 4: Train the actuator-net on the 80% train split ───────────────────
train_actuator_net(
    real_data_path=_train_split_path,
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    hidden_dim=32,
    num_layers=2,
    num_epochs=200,
    output_dir="workspace/calibration/franka_actuator_net_calibrated",
)
# Returns: {checkpoint_path: str, final_loss: float, training_epochs: int,
#           per_joint_rmse: {joint_name: float}} — note: this is the TRAIN RMSE,
# not the validation RMSE. The validation pass in Step 6 produces the held-out figure.
# SUCCESS: checkpoint_path is non-null.

# ── Step 5: Persist optimizer state for resumability ────────────────────────
checkpoint_training(
    tag="post_actuator_net_calibration",
)
# Returns: {checkpoint_file: str, run_id: str, tag: str}

# ── Step 6: Status snapshot — confirms training completed without subprocess crash ─
_status = get_training_status(
    run_id="actuator_net_calibration",
)
# Returns: {state: 'finished'|'running'|'crashed', latest_reward, latest_step, ...}
# GATE: state == 'finished'; if 'crashed', inspect the launcher log before validation.

# ── Step 7: Validate on the 20% held-out test split ─────────────────────────
# This is the differentiating step vs A62 (CP-NEW-actuator-net-training).
# The held-out partition was NEVER seen during training (split by episode_id).
_val_result = validate_calibration(
    calibrated_params={
        "checkpoint_path": "workspace/actuator_net/checkpoint.pt",
        "model_type": "actuator_net_lstm",
        "hidden_dim": 32,
        "num_layers": 2,
    },
    test_data_path=_test_split_path,
)
# Returns: {per_joint_rmse: {joint_0..6: float}, mean_rmse: float, ee_cartesian_rmse: float}
# PRIMARY SUCCESS CRITERION: max(per_joint_rmse.values()) <= 0.05 rad on all 7 joints.
# LEAKAGE GUARD: for each joint, validate per_joint_rmse[j] <= 2 * train_rmse[j];
# a >2x gap indicates within-trajectory leakage in the split.

# ── Step 8 (optional): Fit parametric damping/friction as a cross-check ─────
# Complementary to the neural fit. If only the actuator-net is needed, skip this.
calibrate_physics(
    real_data_path=_train_split_path,
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    parameters_to_calibrate=["friction", "damping"],
    num_samples=20,
    num_workers=2,
    output_dir="workspace/calibration/franka_actuator_net_parametric_residual",
)
# Returns: {calibrated_parameters: {...}, rmse_train: float}
# Compare rmse_train against _val_result.mean_rmse — if the parametric residual
# already matches the actuator-net validation RMSE, the LSTM is not adding value.

# ── Step 9: Smoke-test the calibrated model in a policy-eval rollout ────────
# Confirms the actuator-net checkpoint installs into the env without crashing.
# Not a performance gate — the rollout is short, just enough to fire the policy step.
_smoke_eval = evaluate_reward(
    reward_code="def compute_reward(obs, action, next_obs):\n    return -float(((next_obs['joint_pos'] - obs['joint_pos']) ** 2).sum())\n",
    env_id="Isaac-Reach-Franka-Direct-v0",
    num_steps=200,
)
# Returns: {fitness: float, components: {...}, task_success_rate: float}
# GATE: evaluate_reward must complete without raising; fitness value is informational.

# ── Step 10: Sim-vs-real gap report using the calibrated model ──────────────
# Replays the 20% test trajectories through the calibrated sim and compares to
# the held-out real measurements. Surfaces any residual gap the actuator-net misses.
_gap_report = measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/franka_sim_replay_calibrated.hdf5",
    real_trajectory=_test_split_path,
)
# Returns: {per_joint_errors, ee_cartesian_error, obs_distribution_gap}

# ── Step 11: Prioritized adjustments for joints exceeding the threshold ─────
_adjustments = suggest_parameter_adjustment(
    gap_report=_gap_report,
)
# Returns: [{parameter, direction, magnitude, priority}] — human operator applies top-N.
# Joints with validation RMSE > 0.05 rad should appear at the top of this list.

diagnose_args.success_criterion: train_result.checkpoint_path is not null AND val_result.mean_rmse <= 0.05 AND max(val_result.per_joint_rmse.values()) <= 0.05 AND all(val_result.per_joint_rmse[j] <= 2 * train_result.per_joint_rmse[j] for j in val_result.per_joint_rmse)

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓; function-gate BLOCKED (requires live Isaac Sim + IsaacLab ML backend for train_actuator_net + a real-or-mock CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_hardware_log_mock.csv; the mock CSV is intended to ship with the canonical so the load-and-split path executes locally without a physical Franka)
failure_modes: CSV format mismatch: the mock/real log MUST contain columns timestamp_s, joint_{0..6}_pos, joint_{0..6}_vel, joint_{0..6}_tau, episode_id. A Franka teleop export from a different toolchain (e.g. franka_ros vs libfranka direct) may use joint_position_0 or q_0 or panda_joint1 naming — re-map columns to the expected schema BEFORE calling create_calibration_experiment, or the split handler will write empty HDF5 partitions and train_actuator_net will see zero samples.

Train/test split by row (instead of by episode_id): if the split strategy picks every 5th row for the test set, the LSTM trivially predicts position(t) from position(t-1) because consecutive samples leak across partitions. The diagnose_args leakage guard (test_rmse <= 2 * train_rmse per joint) will catch this — if it fires, switch to episode-id splitting or trajectory-segment splitting.

Validation RMSE too high (model under-fits): if per_joint_rmse > 0.05 rad on the test set, either (a) the training data lacks excitation diversity (single-direction trajectories cannot identify friction asymmetry), (b) num_epochs=200 is too few for the dataset size, or (c) hidden_dim=32 is too small for the joint nonlinearity. Diagnose by inspecting train_result.per_joint_rmse: if train RMSE is also high, the model is under-fitting; if train is low but test is high, the split is leaking or the dataset is too small.

Actuator-net training diverges (NaN loss): insufficient data (<5000 samples after the 80/20 split) or excessive learning rate. train_actuator_net handles NaN by aborting and returning checkpoint_path=null. If this fires, log more excitation trajectories (target 5-10 min of diverse motion at 200 Hz) and retry.

Mock CSV not representative of real hardware: the synthetic friction model in the mock CSV is a coarse approximation — real Franka logs include cogging-torque ripple, gravity-compensation offsets, and joint-specific stiction the mock does not reproduce. The mock is for locally-runnable form-gate verification ONLY; never deploy a model trained on the mock CSV to a real robot.

get_training_status with wrong run_id: the run_id must match the launcher's bookkeeping. train_actuator_net stores its checkpoints under the output_dir derived from the articulation name; pass run_id='actuator_net_calibration' verbatim to match. If the call returns state='unknown', the run already completed and the launcher state was cleared — this is safe to ignore.

validate_calibration calibrated_params shape: for actuator-net, the dict carries {'checkpoint_path': ..., 'model_type': 'actuator_net_lstm', 'hidden_dim': int, 'num_layers': int}. The handler dispatches on model_type to choose actuator-net loading vs parametric-physics loading. Wrong model_type silently falls back to parametric, producing nonsense RMSE.

measure_sim_real_gap when sim replay is stale: the sim_trajectory path must be REGENERATED after the actuator-net is loaded — otherwise the gap reflects pre-calibration sim physics and the suggest_parameter_adjustment output is misleading. The canonical lists this path but does not regenerate it automatically; downstream automation must invoke the replay before this step.

calibrate_physics num_samples=20 with num_workers=2: this is a quick parametric cross-check (~5-10 min). Increasing num_samples to 100 + num_workers to 4 yields a fuller Bayesian fit (30-60 min). On a single-GPU workstation, leave at 20/2 to keep the canonical interactive.

evaluate_reward env_id mismatch: 'Isaac-Reach-Franka-Direct-v0' is the standard reach task; alternative envs (Isaac-Lift-Cube-Franka-v0, Isaac-Pickplace-Franka-v0) have different obs schemas and the smoke-test reward_code will raise a KeyError. Verify env_id against list_extensions output before substituting.
extension_notes: First canonical to exercise actuator-net CALIBRATION (load real hardware log + train/test split + held-out validation), as opposed to from-scratch training (CP-NEW-actuator-net-training, A62) or parametric Bayesian fit (CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator). Builds on the rl-calibrate-physics-actuator pattern but substitutes a learned LSTM motor model for the parametric friction/damping fit. The mock CSV at workspace/calibration/franka_hardware_log_mock.csv ships with the canonical so the LOAD-and-SPLIT path executes locally without a physical Franka. Real-log users substitute their own CSV path verbatim. Pairs with CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (cross-check parametric vs neural) and CP-NEW-sim2real-gap-injection (downstream sensitivity sweep on the calibrated model).
code_template:
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="train_test_split_strategy",
    range=[0.0, 1.0],
    real_data_path={{hardware_log.path}},
    num_samples=2,
)

_effort_report = monitor_joint_effort(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    duration_seconds=2.0,
)

train_actuator_net(
    real_data_path={{hardware_log.train_split_path}},
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    hidden_dim={{training.hidden_dim}},
    num_layers={{training.num_layers}},
    num_epochs={{training.num_epochs}},
    output_dir={{training.output_dir}},
)

checkpoint_training(
    tag={{checkpoint.tag}},
)

_status = get_training_status(
    run_id={{checkpoint.run_id}},
)

_val_result = validate_calibration(
    calibrated_params={
        "checkpoint_path": "workspace/actuator_net/checkpoint.pt",
        "model_type": "actuator_net_lstm",
        "hidden_dim": {{training.hidden_dim}},
        "num_layers": {{training.num_layers}},
    },
    test_data_path={{hardware_log.test_split_path}},
)

calibrate_physics(
    real_data_path={{hardware_log.train_split_path}},
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    parameters_to_calibrate={{parametric_cross_check.parameters}},
    num_samples={{parametric_cross_check.num_samples}},
    num_workers={{parametric_cross_check.num_workers}},
    output_dir={{parametric_cross_check.output_dir}},
)

_smoke_eval = evaluate_reward(
    reward_code="def compute_reward(obs, action, next_obs):\n    return -float(((next_obs['joint_pos'] - obs['joint_pos']) ** 2).sum())\n",
    env_id={{smoke_eval.env_id}},
    num_steps={{smoke_eval.num_steps}},
)

_gap_report = measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_trajectory={{gap_report.sim_trajectory_path}},
    real_trajectory={{hardware_log.test_split_path}},
)

_adjustments = suggest_parameter_adjustment(
    gap_report=_gap_report,
)
